svg.title: Firebase crop migration and plant expansion roadmap
svg.desc: Detailed roadmap for migrating crop files from GitHub to Firestore, adding a migration script, expanding to trees and ornamentals, and updating the admin UI.

g.text: Data model design — do this first

svg.text: One document per crop — fields mirror Crop.js constructor exactly (id, name, cat, emoji, planting, water, fertilizer, …)
New top-level fields to add now (before migration, cheaper than later)
Complexity: low — schema planning only; output a schema doc before writing any code

g.text: One-time migration script — GitHub JS → Firestore

svg.text: Run once, locally, with a service account key (never ship the key)
Node.js script: list GitHub /crops/*.js → parse each file → writeBatch() to Firestore
Firestore security rules for the crops collection
Complexity: medium — one-time effort; the parseCropFile() function already exists in index.html

g.text: Update index.html — read from Firestore instead of GitHub

svg.text: Replace loadAllCrops() GitHub fetch with Firestore query
getDocs(collection(db, 'crops')) → no API rate limits, no token, instant load
Query by type / cat (no client filter)
loadCrops() in garden.html uses the same GitHub fetch — replace with the same Firestore query
Complexity: low — swap one async function; Firestore SDK already loaded in garden.html

g.text: Admin UI — add / edit crops directly in the browser

svg.text: saveEdit() already has the form — wire it to Firestore instead of the dummy _doSave()
Edit modal additions for trees / perennials
Conditional form sections (show only when relevant)
Complexity: medium — form already exists; main work is Firestore write + admin auth check

g.text: Plant library expansion — trees, shrubs, ornamentals

svg.text: Suggested new categories to add (enter via admin UI from phase C)
Add type filter above category row
Complexity: low per plant — bulk entry via admin UI; schema already handles all types after phase 0
